title: Data Vision
text:
Objective
Establish data as a strategic banking asset by:

Ensuring regulatory compliance

Improving operational efficiency

Enabling trusted decision-making

Accelerating AI-driven innovation across the bank

title: Data Vision
text:
My Vision
Build a trusted, governed, scalable, and business-driven data organization that connects:

Data Governance

Enterprise Data Platform

Analytics & Data Science


one operating model for the bank

title: Data Vision
text:
Banking is Different
Compared with other industries, banking data must support:


Regulatory compliance

Data lineage and auditability

Confidentiality and controlled access

Risk transparency

Near real-time operational visibility

Consistent KPI definitions

title: Data Vision
text:
High-Level Architecture





flowchart LR

A[Data Sources] --> B[Data Platform]
B --> C[Data Consumption]

D[Data Governance Layer] --- A
D --- B
D --- C

E[API / Services Layer] --- A
E --- B
E --- C

title: Data Vision
text:
Block 1 | Data Sources & Core Systems





flowchart LR

A1[Core Banking Systems] --> B[Ingestion Layer]
A2[CRM Systems] --> B
A3[Payments Systems] --> B
A4[Digital Channels] --> B
A5[External Data Sources] --> B
A6[Streaming Data] --> B

title: Data Vision
text:
Components

Core Banking (accounts, transactions)

CRM & Customer Data

Payments & Cards Systems

Mobile / Web Applications

External APIs (credit bureau, fintechs)

Event Streams (real-time transactions)

title: Data Vision
text:
Block 2 | Data Platform (Lakehouse)





flowchart LR

B1[Ingestion Layer] --> B2[Raw Data Lake]
B2 --> B3[Processing Layer]
B3 --> B4[Data Warehouse]
B4 --> B5[Data Marts / Feature Store]

title: Data Vision
text:
Layers
Ingestion

Batch ETL pipelines

Streaming ingestion (Kafka)

Raw Layer

Unstructured & semi-structured data

Immutable storage

3. Processing Layer

Data transformations

Standardization & cleansing

4. Data Warehouse

Structured, curated datasets

Business-ready data

5. Serving Layer

Data marts

Feature stores for ML

Data Analytics

title: Data Vision
text:
Block 3 | Data Governance Layer





flowchart TB

G1[Data Quality]
G2[Data Lineage]
G3[Metadata Management]
G4[Master Data Management]
G5[Security & Access Control]
G6[Regulatory Compliance]
G7[Audit & Monitoring]

title: Data Vision
text:
Capabilities

Data validation and reconciliation

End-to-end lineage tracking

Centralized metadata catalog

Golden customer/product records (MDM)

Role-based access control

Compliance (GDPR, Basel, etc.)

Full auditability

title: Data Vision
text:
Block 4 | Analytics, Data Science & AI





flowchart LR

C1[BI & Reporting] --> C5[Decision Layer]
C2[Advanced Analytics] --> C5
C3[Data Science Models] --> C5
C4[AI Systems] --> C5

title: Data Vision
text:
Layers
1. BI & Reporting

Dashboards (Tableau, Power BI)
Regulatory reporting

2. Advanced Analytics

SQL-based analytics
Python-based analysis

3. Data Science

Churn prediction
Fraud detection
Credit risk models

4. AI Layer

Recommendation systems
LLM-based assistants

5. Decision Layer

Campaign management
Automation systems
API-driven decisioning

title: Data Vision
text:
Components

Open Banking APIs

Internal data services

Microservices architecture

Fintech integrations
